Asar — اثر (Traces)

Asar (اثر, meaning "traces" or "heritage") is a digital archive of Tooba, a Pashto-language magazine published in Peshawar between 1928 and 1939. This viewer makes the full text of 282 magazine spreads searchable for the first time.

The magazine covered politics, literature, religion, education, and daily life under British colonial rule in the North-West Frontier Province. It was an important voice for Pashto literacy and cultural identity.

Methodology: Each spread was photographed, reduced to JPEG, and transcribed using Google Gemini's multimodal OCR. Results were verified with a second LLM pass and manually reviewed. Two images were permanently unreadable and excluded.

Search: Uses TF-IDF over all 282 pages. You can search in Pashto script or English (common keywords are translated automatically).

Timeline: Shows how frequently a word appears across each issue, normalised per 10 pages.

Map: Geographic names were detected from a curated Pashto gazetteer and mapped to coordinates.

Topics: Pages were clustered using TF-IDF + K-Means, reduced to 2D with PCA for visualisation.

Digitisation project by Tooba. Archive viewer built with Claude Code. Images and OCR data hosted on GitHub.
